uplink.description: GNSS fix while moving, with battery and temperature
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds payload (lat 35.123456, lon -101.654321 both in WGS84 range). Field values cross-checked by running reference/upstream-codec.js: {battery:55, temperature:21.4, latitude:35.123456, longitude:-101.654321, motion_status:'moving', geofence_status:'inside'}. Normalized to vocabulary: battery%->batteryPercent, temperature->air.temperature, lat/lon->position, moving->action.motion.detected=true.

uplink.description: Wi-Fi scan result (one AP) while stopped, with battery
uplink._note: Synthetic Wi-Fi positioning payload. Upstream reference run yields {battery:80, wifi_scan_result:'finish', motion_status:'stop', wifi:[{group:1, mac:'24:6f:28:12:34:56', rssi:-67, motion_status:'stop'}]}. 'stop' is a real motion state, so action.motion.detected=false; the scan list is the camelCase extra wifiScan[].

TTN catalog example input (at101-codec.yaml). Upstream decodes device_status/ipso/hardware/firmware/sn (the 0xff/0x0b status byte, then 0xff/0x01 IPSO, 0xff/0x16 SN, 0xff/0x09 HW, 0xff/0x0a FW). All emitted as camelCase device-info extras (no measurement vocabulary applies).

Synthetic 0x20/0xCE historical-location TLV. Upstream reference run yields history:[{timestamp:1719000000, longitude:-3.7, latitude:40.5}]; emitted as the camelCase extra locationHistory[] (a single-object decode cannot use the top-level position for a past fix).
Synthetic payload with an unknown channel/type so the decoder recognizes nothing and returns an error.
